LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, APR-28-2021 HEARING HELD RE: RE: PLAINTIFF JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION AND PAGA SETTLEMENT. THE COURT AND PARTIES DISCUSS THE ISSUES OUTLINED IN THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND DISCUSS A SCHEDULE FOR THE SUBMISSION OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS AND A FURTHER HEARING. THE COURT INSTRUCTS THE PARTIES TO FILE THEIR SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING AND FACTUAL SHOWING BY MAY 7, 2021. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MATTER FOR FURTHER HEARING TO MAY 18, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE SECOND SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, AS WELL AS THE DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL, AND THEY PROVIDE SUFFICIENT DETAILED INFORMATION AND ANALYSIS TO WARRANT PRELIMINARY APPROVAL. FINAL FAIRNESS HEARING IS SET FOR AUGUST 26, 2021 AT 9:30AM, DEPT 302. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Attorney Fees, Costs, And Enhancement Award
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. GRAHAM B. LIPPSMITH, Esq. and CELENE CHAN ANDREWS, Esq. of LippSmith LLP (Tel: 213-344-1820) for plaintiff;
Atty. JEFFREY P. FUCHSMAN, Esq. of Ballard Rosenberg Golper & Savitt, LLP (Tel: 818-508-3700) for defendants Galleher LLC and Galleher Corporation.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Attorney Fees, Costs, And Enhancement Award.

Matter discussed and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for final approval of class action settlement, attorney fees, costs, and enhancement payment is granted on the following conditions: (1) the award of attorneys' fees to class counsel shall be in the amount of $637,583.33, representing thirty-three percent of the gross settlement fund; and (2) the enhancement to the named plaintiff shall be in the amount of $5,000. The Court finds these amounts to be reasonable in the circumstances. The net difference between the amounts sought and those awarded shall be included in the net settlement fund to be distributed to class members.

Single order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Final Fairness Hearing.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear to allow class members to appear.

The Court notes that no class members are present at today's hearing.

The Court and counsel agree to the following dates/deadlines:
- 10/25/21: settlement effective date;
- 11/4/21: settlement funding deadline;
- 11/18/21: deadline for checks to be mailed;
- 3/18/21: deadline for checks to be cashed;
- 3/20/21: deadline for filing of disbursement declaration;
- 4/13/22 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 for Compliance Hearing

Single order signed.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement:
Plaintiff's motion for preliminary approval of class action and PAGA settlement, as amended by the May 7, 2021 addendum, is granted. The Court has reviewed the second supplemental brief and declaration filed by plaintiff's counsel, as well as the declaration filed by defendants' counsel, and they provide sufficient detailed information and analysis to warrant preliminary approval. Final fairness hearing is set for August 26, 2021 at 9:30am, Dept 302.

Plaintiff Jesus Diaz Sanchez's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement.

The matter is called. The Court notes that it appears that counsel does not want to renotice but would like to set a continued hearing in this matter.

DISCUSSIONS are held. At the conclusion of discussions, Plaintiff Jesus Diaz Sanchez's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement is continued to June 7, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302.

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Apr-28-2021 Continued To May-18-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. GRAHAM B. LIPPSMITH, Esq. and CELENE CHAN ANDREWS, Esq. of LippSmith LLP (Tel: 213-344-1820) for plaintiff;
Atty. JEFFREY P. FUCHSMAN, Esq. of Ballard Rosenberg Golper & Savitt, LLP (Tel: 818-508-3700) for defendants Galleher LLC and Galleher Corporation.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement.

The Court and parties discuss the issues outlined in the Court's tentative ruling and discuss a schedule for the submission of the supplemental papers and a further hearing.

Per the Court's tentative ruling, the Court has indicated as follows: Plaintiff has not presented sufficient information to enable the Court to evaluate the reasonableness of the proposed settlement. In order to determine whether a proposed class action settlement is fair and reasonable, a trial court "should consider relevant factors, such as the strength of plaintiffs' case, the risk, expense, complexity and likely duration of further litigation, the risk of maintaining class action status through trial, the amount offered in settlement, the extent of discovery completed and the stage of the proceedings, the experience and views of counsel, the presence of a governmental participant, and the reaction of the class members to the proposed settlement." (Dunk v. Ford Motor Co. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1801.) "'The most important factor is the strength of the case for plaintiffs on the merits, balanced against the amount offered in settlement.'" (Kullar v. Foot Locker Retail, Inc. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 116, 130.) An initial presumption of fairness exists if certain factors are present, but "an informed evaluation cannot be made without an understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation." (Id. at 120; accord, Munoz v. BCI Coca-Cola Bottling Co. of Los Angeles (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 399, 409 [Kullar does not mandate "an explicit statement of the maximum amount the plaintiff class could recover if it prevailed on all its claims," but it does require "a record which allows 'an understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation.'"]; Clark v. American Residential Services LLC (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 785, 790, 801-803 [vacating order approving settlement of wage and hour settlement where trial court lacked sufficient information to make an informed evaluation of the fairness of the settlement].)

Here, while the settlement amount is undeniably substantial, plaintiff's moving papers lack any meaningful discussion of the amount that is in controversy, the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation, the merits of plaintiffs' claims, the potential defenses, or other factors taken into account by the parties in negotiating a settlement. The discussion in counsel's declarations is far too general and conclusory to provide the Court with a sufficient basis on which to make the requisite finding. (E.g., Aiwazian Decl. pars. 18-28; Lippsmith Decl. pars. 25-28.)

The Court instructs the parties to file their supplemental briefing and factual showing by May 7, 2021.

The Court continues this matter for further hearing to May 18, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
